Every virtual asset service provider shall at all times— (a) provide services honestly and fairly; (b) maintain and hold the prescribed capital requirements; (c) manage any actual and potential conflicts of interest related to its services; (d) have adequate technological, financial and human resources to discharge its services; (e) comply with the full range of AML/CFT/ CPF preventive measures, including targeted financial sanctions obligations; (f) have its annual financial statement audited; (g) open and operate a bank account in Kenya for the purposes of this Act; (h) ensure that recording, storing, protecting and transmission of the data processed by it is in accordance with laws in Kenya; (i) ensure that all marketing and promotional materials are fair, clear, transparent and not misleading; The Virtual Asset Service Providers Bill, 2025 22 (j) plan for business continuity and disaster recovery in the event of an incident or a disaster; (k) have in place a mechanism for handling customer complaints; (l) have in place a mechanism for protecting whistle-blowers; (m) take reasonable steps to prevent market abuse and ensure the integrity and transparency of the financial market; (n) take reasonable steps and measures to maintain an effective consumer education program for the virtual asset in which it deals; (o) take reasonable steps to ensure that employees and persons acting on its behalf comply with the provisions of this Act or any other relevant law; (p) maintain competence to provide the virtual asset services; (q) if offering for sale a virtual asset, conduct due diligence on the virtual asset and its issuer, taking into account the requirements of this Act and any other relevant law; (r) take reasonable steps to ensure that the beneficial owners are fit and proper; (s) take reasonable steps to ensure that its beneficial owners or natural person employed by, or associated with, the virtual asset service providers comply with this Act and all applicable laws in Kenya; (t) take reasonable steps to ensure that its beneficial owners or natural person employed by, or associated with, the virtual asset service providers comply with the code of conduct of virtual asset service provider’s set out by the relevant regulatory authority;
